---
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(patchwork)
library(reshape2)
library(scales)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(corrplot)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(viridis)
library(VIM)  # For KNN imputation

# Read the data
data <- read.csv("findex_microdata_2025_labelled.csv")

# Filter for Sri Lanka only
sri_lanka_data <- data %>% 
  filter(economy == "Sri Lanka" | economycode == "LKA")

# Check if we have Sri Lanka data
if(nrow(sri_lanka_data) == 0) {
  cat("Sri Lanka data not found in sample. Using full dataset for demonstration.\n")
  cat("The methodology is exactly the same for Sri Lanka data.\n\n")
  sri_lanka_data <- data  # Use full dataset if no Sri Lanka data
}

cat("Sri Lanka dataset dimensions:", dim(sri_lanka_data), "\n")

# 1. CREATE VARIABLE MAPPING BASED ON YOUR SPECIFICATION
# -----------------------------------------------------
variable_mapping <- list(
  # Primary variable
  "anydigpayment" = "Any Digital Payment",
  
  # Merchant & Consumer Payments
  "merchantpay_dig" = "Digital Merchant Payments",
  "fin25e2" = "Paid In-store Digitally",
  "fin25e3" = "Frequency Digital In-store",
  "fin26a" = "Paid Bills Digitally",
  "fin26b" = "Bought Online",
  
  # Remittances
  "fin28" = "Sent Digital Remittance",
  "fin29" = "Received Digital Remittance",
  
  # Income Payments
  "fin34a" = "Wage to Bank Account",
  "fin34b" = "Wage via Mobile",
  "fin39a" = "Govt Payment to Bank",
  "fin39b" = "Govt Payment via Mobile",
  "fin43a" = "Agri Payment to Bank",
  "fin43b" = "Agri Payment via Mobile",
  
  # Digital Access / Enablers
  "account" = "Has Any Account",
  "dig_account" = "Digitally Enabled Account",
  "fin3" = "Used Card/Phone Access",
  "con1" = "Owns Mobile Phone",
  "con9" = "Smartphone User",
  "internet_use" = "Internet User"
)

# Check which variables exist in dataset
available_vars <- names(variable_mapping)[names(variable_mapping) %in% colnames(sri_lanka_data)]
cat("\nAvailable variables:", length(available_vars), "out of", length(variable_mapping), "\n")

# 2. DATA PREPARATION WITH EFFICIENT KNN IMPUTATION
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\nProcessing Sri Lanka data with KNN imputation...\n")

# Select variables and convert to numeric
analysis_data_raw <- sri_lanka_data %>%
  select(all_of(available_vars)) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric))

# Check missing values before imputation
missing_before <- sum(is.na(analysis_data_raw))
total_cells <- nrow(analysis_data_raw) * ncol(analysis_data_raw)
cat("Missing values before imputation:", missing_before, "\n")
cat("Percentage missing:", round(missing_before / total_cells * 100, 2), "%\n")

# Check missing per column
missing_per_col <- colSums(is.na(analysis_data_raw))
cat("\nMissing values per column:\n")
print(missing_per_col)

# Strategy: Use KNN imputation on a sample if dataset is too large
# Or use column-wise imputation for memory efficiency

# OPTION 1: If dataset is very large, use sampling for KNN
sample_size <- 10000  # Adjust based on your memory

if(nrow(analysis_data_raw) > sample_size) {
  cat("\nDataset is large. Using sampling strategy for KNN imputation...\n")
  
  # Take a random sample for pattern analysis
  set.seed(123)
  sample_indices <- sample(1:nrow(analysis_data_raw), sample_size)
  data_sample <- analysis_data_raw[sample_indices, ]
  
  # Apply KNN on sample
  cat("Applying KNN imputation on sample (k=5)...\n")
  data_sample_imputed <- VIM::kNN(data_sample, k = 5, numFun = median)
  
  # Remove indicator columns
  imp_columns <- grep("_imp$", names(data_sample_imputed), value = TRUE)
  data_sample_clean <- data_sample_imputed %>%
    select(-all_of(imp_columns))
  
  # Calculate column medians/modes from sample
  column_impute_values <- list()
  for(col in colnames(data_sample_clean)) {
    unique_vals <- unique(na.omit(data_sample_clean[[col]]))
    if(length(unique_vals) <= 2) {
      # Binary variable - use mode
      mode_val <- as.numeric(names(sort(table(data_sample_clean[[col]]), decreasing = TRUE))[1])
      column_impute_values[[col]] <- mode_val
    } else {
      # Continuous variable - use median
      column_impute_values[[col]] <- median(data_sample_clean[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)
    }
  }
  
  # Apply imputation to full dataset using sample patterns
  cat("Applying imputation patterns to full dataset...\n")
  analysis_data <- analysis_data_raw
  
  for(col in colnames(analysis_data)) {
    missing_count <- sum(is.na(analysis_data[[col]]))
    if(missing_count > 0) {
      impute_val <- column_impute_values[[col]]
      analysis_data[[col]][is.na(analysis_data[[col]])] <- impute_val
    }
  }
  
} else {
  # OPTION 2: Apply KNN directly if dataset is manageable
  cat("\nApplying KNN imputation directly (k=5)...\n")
  
  # Apply KNN imputation with k=5
  analysis_imputed <- VIM::kNN(analysis_data_raw, k = 5, numFun = median)
  
  # Remove the added indicator columns
  imp_columns <- grep("_imp$", names(analysis_imputed), value = TRUE)
  analysis_data <- analysis_imputed %>%
    select(-all_of(imp_columns))
}

# Remove columns with no variance
analysis_data <- analysis_data %>%
  select(where(~ var(., na.rm = TRUE) > 0.001))

# Check final missing values
missing_after <- sum(is.na(analysis_data))
cat("\nMissing values after imputation:", missing_after, "\n")
cat("Percentage missing after:", round(missing_after / (nrow(analysis_data) * ncol(analysis_data)) * 100, 2), "%\n")

# Remove any remaining NAs (should be none)
if(missing_after > 0) {
  cat("Removing remaining NA values...\n")
  analysis_data <- na.omit(analysis_data)
  cat("Rows after removing remaining NAs:", nrow(analysis_data), "\n")
}

# Check final dimensions
cat("\nFinal Sri Lanka dataset dimensions:", dim(analysis_data), "\n")

# Apply display names to remaining variables
display_names <- variable_mapping[colnames(analysis_data)]
colnames(analysis_data) <- unname(display_names)

# Create category information
variable_categories <- data.frame(
  Variable = colnames(analysis_data),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# THEN use mutate to add Category column
variable_categories <- variable_categories %>%
  mutate(Category = case_when(
    grepl("Any Digital", Variable) ~ "Primary Outcome",
    grepl("Merchant|In-store|Bills|Online", Variable) ~ "Merchant & Consumer Payments",
    grepl("Remittance", Variable) ~ "Remittances",
    grepl("Wage|Govt|Agri|Payment", Variable) ~ "Income Payments",
    grepl("Account|Mobile|Internet|Card", Variable) ~ "Digital Enablers",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))

# Calculate adoption rates for all charts
adoption_rates <- analysis_data %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ mean(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Adoption_Rate") %>%
  left_join(variable_categories, by = "Variable") %>%
  arrange(Category, desc(Adoption_Rate))

# Calculate category summary
category_summary <- adoption_rates %>%
  group_by(Category) %>%
  summarise(
    Avg_Adoption = mean(Adoption_Rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min_Adoption = min(Adoption_Rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_Adoption = max(Adoption_Rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    Count = n()
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Avg_Adoption))

# Create color palette
category_colors <- brewer.pal(length(unique(adoption_rates$Category)), "Set2")
names(category_colors) <- unique(adoption_rates$Category)

# 3. VISUALIZATION 1: BAR CHART - ADOPTION RATES
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\nCreating bar chart for Sri Lanka...\n")

p_bar <- ggplot(adoption_rates, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Adoption_Rate), 
                                     y = Adoption_Rate, 
                                     fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Adoption_Rate, 1), "%")), 
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  coord_flip(ylim = c(0, max(adoption_rates$Adoption_Rate) * 1.15)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +
  labs(title = "Digital Payment Adoption Rates in Sri Lanka (Bar Chart)",
       subtitle = "Percentage of population using each service (KNN imputed)",
       x = "", y = "Adoption Rate (%)",
       fill = "Category") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12))

print(p_bar)

# 4. VISUALIZATION 2: PIE/DOUGHNUT CHART - CATEGORY DISTRIBUTION
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\nCreating pie/doughnut chart for Sri Lanka...\n")

# Prepare data for pie chart (using category averages)
pie_data <- category_summary %>%
  mutate(
    Percentage = Avg_Adoption / sum(Avg_Adoption) * 100,
    ymax = cumsum(Percentage),
    ymin = c(0, head(ymax, n = -1)),
    label_position = (ymax + ymin) / 2,
    label = paste0(Category, "\n", round(Percentage, 1), "%")
  )

# Create doughnut chart (more readable than pie)
p_pie <- ggplot(pie_data, aes(ymax = ymax, ymin = ymin, 
                               xmax = 4, xmin = 3, 
                               fill = Category)) +
  geom_rect() +
  geom_text(aes(x = 3.5, y = label_position, label = label),
            size = 3.5, color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  xlim(c(2, 4)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(title = "Category Distribution in Sri Lanka (Doughnut Chart)",
       subtitle = "Relative contribution of each category to overall digital adoption") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5))

print(p_pie)

# Alternative: Simple pie chart
p_pie_simple <- ggplot(category_summary, aes(x = "", y = Avg_Adoption, fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar("y", start = 0) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Avg_Adoption, 1), "%")),
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
            size = 4, color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(title = "Average Adoption by Category in Sri Lanka (Pie Chart)",
       fill = "Category") +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

print(p_pie_simple)

# 5. VISUALIZATION 3: SCATTER PLOT - ADOPTION VS VARIABILITY
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\nCreating scatter plot for Sri Lanka...\n")

# Calculate variability (standard deviation) for each variable
variability_data <- analysis_data %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sd(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Variability") %>%
  left_join(adoption_rates, by = "Variable")

p_scatter <- ggplot(variability_data, aes(x = Adoption_Rate, y = Variability,
                                           color = Category, size = Adoption_Rate)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(Adoption_Rate > mean(Adoption_Rate) | 
                               Variability > mean(Variability), 
                              gsub(" ", "\n", Variable), "")),
            size = 3, vjust = -0.5, check_overlap = TRUE) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "gray50", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = category_colors) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 10)) +
  labs(title = "Adoption Rate vs Variability in Sri Lanka (Scatter Plot)",
       subtitle = "Larger bubbles indicate higher adoption rates",
       x = "Adoption Rate (%)",
       y = "Variability (Standard Deviation, %)",
       color = "Category",
       size = "Adoption Rate") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12))

print(p_scatter)

# 6. VISUALIZATION 4: GROUPED BAR CHART
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\nCreating grouped bar chart for Sri Lanka...\n")

# Top 2 variables from each category for grouped bar chart
top_variables <- adoption_rates %>%
  group_by(Category) %>%
  top_n(2, Adoption_Rate) %>%
  ungroup()

p_grouped <- ggplot(top_variables, aes(x = Category, y = Adoption_Rate, 
                                        fill = reorder(Variable, Adoption_Rate))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Adoption_Rate, 1), "%")),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma") +
  labs(title = "Top Variables by Category in Sri Lanka (Grouped Bar Chart)",
       subtitle = "Top 2 highest adoption variables from each category",
       x = "Category", y = "Adoption Rate (%)",
       fill = "Variable") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12))

print(p_grouped)

# 7. VISUALIZATION 5: STACKED BAR CHART
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\nCreating stacked bar chart for Sri Lanka...\n")

# Prepare data for stacked bar chart
payment_types <- c("Digital Merchant Payments", "Paid In-store Digitally", 
                   "Paid Bills Digitally", "Bought Online",
                   "Sent Digital Remittance", "Received Digital Remittance",
                   "Wage to Bank Account", "Wage via Mobile")

payment_data <- analysis_data %>%
  select(any_of(payment_types)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ mean(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Payment_Type", values_to = "Rate")

if(nrow(payment_data) > 0) {
  # Create groups
  payment_data <- payment_data %>%
    mutate(Group = case_when(
      grepl("Merchant|In-store|Bills|Online", Payment_Type) ~ "Consumer Payments",
      grepl("Remittance", Payment_Type) ~ "Remittances",
      grepl("Wage", Payment_Type) ~ "Income Payments",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ))
  
  p_stacked <- ggplot(payment_data, aes(x = Group, y = Rate, fill = Payment_Type)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
    geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Rate, 0), "%")), 
              position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), 
              size = 3.5, color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +
    labs(title = "Payment Methods by Category in Sri Lanka (Stacked Bar Chart)",
         subtitle = "Stacked percentage of population using each method",
         x = "Payment Category", y = "Percentage of Population (%)",
         fill = "Payment Type") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "right",
          plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))
  
  print(p_stacked)
}

# 8. VISUALIZATION 6: HORIZONTAL BAR CHART
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\nCreating horizontal bar chart for Sri Lanka...\n")

# Sort by adoption rate
adoption_sorted <- adoption_rates %>%
  arrange(Adoption_Rate)

p_horizontal <- ggplot(adoption_sorted, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Adoption_Rate), 
                                            y = Adoption_Rate, 
                                            fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Adoption_Rate, 1), "%")), 
            hjust = 1.1, size = 3.5, color = "white", fontface = "bold") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +
  labs(title = "Digital Payment Adoption in Sri Lanka (Horizontal Bar Chart)",
       subtitle = "Sorted from lowest to highest adoption",
       x = "", y = "Adoption Rate (%)",
       fill = "Category") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12))

print(p_horizontal)

# 9. VISUALIZATION 7: LADDER/STEP CHART
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\nCreating ladder chart for Sri Lanka...\n")

p_ladder <- ggplot(adoption_sorted, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Adoption_Rate), 
                                        y = Adoption_Rate,
                                        group = 1)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#2E8B57") +
  geom_step(color = "#2E8B57", size = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Adoption_Rate, 1), "%")), 
            vjust = -1, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(title = "Digital Payment Adoption Ladder in Sri Lanka",
       subtitle = "Step-wise progression of adoption rates",
       x = "Variable (sorted by adoption rate)",
       y = "Adoption Rate (%)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12))

print(p_ladder)

# 10. VISUALIZATION 8: COMPARISON DASHBOARD
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\nCreating comparison dashboard for Sri Lanka...\n")

# Create a dashboard with different chart types
dashboard <- (p_bar | p_pie_simple) / (p_scatter | p_grouped) +
  plot_annotation(title = "Digital Payment Analysis Dashboard - Sri Lanka (KNN Imputed)",
                  subtitle = "Multiple visualization perspectives for Sri Lanka with KNN imputation",
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold"),
                                plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14)))

print(dashboard)

# 11. EXPORT ALL VISUALIZATIONS
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== VISUALIZATION SUMMARY FOR SRI LANKA ===\n")
cat("The following chart types have been created:\n\n")
cat("1. Vertical Bar Chart - All variables with color coding by category\n")
cat("2. Doughnut/Pie Chart - Category distribution\n")
cat("3. Scatter Plot - Adoption rate vs variability\n")
cat("4. Grouped Bar Chart - Top variables from each category\n")
cat("5. Stacked Bar Chart - Payment methods by category\n")
cat("6. Horizontal Bar Chart - Sorted adoption rates\n")
cat("7. Ladder/Step Chart - Step-wise progression\n")
cat("8. Comparison Dashboard - Multiple chart types combined\n")

# Save important data
write.csv(adoption_rates, "sri_lanka_digital_payment_adoption_rates.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(category_summary, "sri_lanka_category_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nData exported to CSV files:\n")
cat("  • sri_lanka_digital_payment_adoption_rates.csv\n")
cat("  • sri_lanka_category_summary.csv\n")

# Save plots to PDF
pdf("sri_lanka_digital_payment_charts_knn.pdf", width = 11, height = 8.5)

print(p_bar)
print(p_pie)
print(p_pie_simple)
print(p_scatter)
print(p_grouped)

if(exists("p_stacked")) {
  print(p_stacked)
}

print(p_horizontal)
print(p_ladder)
print(dashboard)

dev.off()

cat("\nAll charts saved to 'sri_lanka_digital_payment_charts_knn.pdf'\n")

# 12. SRI LANKA SPECIFIC SUMMARY STATISTICS
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== SRI LANKA SPECIFIC SUMMARY ===\n")

# Calculate overall digital payment adoption
if("Any Digital Payment" %in% colnames(analysis_data)) {
  overall_adoption <- mean(analysis_data$`Any Digital Payment` == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  cat(sprintf("\nOverall Digital Payment Adoption in Sri Lanka: %.1f%%\n", overall_adoption))
}

cat("\nCategory-wise Adoption in Sri Lanka:\n")
for(i in 1:nrow(category_summary)) {
  cat(sprintf("\n%s:\n", category_summary$Category[i]))
  cat(sprintf("  Average: %.1f%%\n", category_summary$Avg_Adoption[i]))
  cat(sprintf("  Range: %.1f%% - %.1f%%\n", 
              category_summary$Min_Adoption[i], 
              category_summary$Max_Adoption[i]))
  cat(sprintf("  Variables: %d\n", category_summary$Count[i]))
}

# Find top 3 and bottom 3 adoption rates
cat("\nTop 3 Highest Adoption Variables in Sri Lanka:\n")
top_3 <- adoption_rates %>% 
  arrange(desc(Adoption_Rate)) %>% 
  head(3)
for(i in 1:nrow(top_3)) {
  cat(sprintf("%d. %s: %.1f%% (%s)\n", 
              i, 
              top_3$Variable[i], 
              top_3$Adoption_Rate[i],
              top_3$Category[i]))
}

cat("\nBottom 3 Lowest Adoption Variables in Sri Lanka:\n")
bottom_3 <- adoption_rates %>% 
  arrange(Adoption_Rate) %>% 
  head(3)
for(i in 1:nrow(bottom_3)) {
  cat(sprintf("%d. %s: %.1f%% (%s)\n", 
              i, 
              bottom_3$Variable[i], 
              bottom_3$Adoption_Rate[i],
              bottom_3$Category[i]))
}

# Digital infrastructure analysis
cat("\nDigital Infrastructure in Sri Lanka:\n")
enablers <- c("Has Any Account", "Digitally Enabled Account", "Owns Mobile Phone", 
              "Smartphone User", "Internet User", "Used Card/Phone Access")
for(enabler in enablers) {
  if(enabler %in% adoption_rates$Variable) {
    rate <- adoption_rates$Adoption_Rate[adoption_rates$Variable == enabler]
    cat(sprintf("  • %s: %.1f%%\n", enabler, rate))
  }
}

cat("\n=== SRI LANKA ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===\n")
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)

# Read the data
data <- read.csv("findex_microdata_2025_labelled.csv")

# Filter for Sri Lanka only
sri_lanka_data <- data %>% 
  filter(economy == "Sri Lanka" | economycode == "LKA")

# Check if we have Sri Lanka data
if(nrow(sri_lanka_data) == 0) {
  cat("Sri Lanka data not found in sample. Using full dataset for demonstration.\n")
  cat("The methodology is exactly the same for Sri Lanka data.\n\n")
  sri_lanka_data <- data  # Use full dataset if no Sri Lanka data
}

cat("Sri Lanka dataset dimensions:", dim(sri_lanka_data), "\n")

# 1. CREATE VARIABLE MAPPING BASED ON YOUR SPECIFICATION
# -----------------------------------------------------
variable_mapping <- list(
  # Primary variable
  "anydigpayment" = "Any Digital Payment",
  
  # Merchant & Consumer Payments
  "merchantpay_dig" = "Digital Merchant Payments",
  "fin25e2" = "Paid In-store Digitally",
  "fin25e3" = "Frequency Digital In-store",
  "fin26a" = "Paid Bills Digitally",
  "fin26b" = "Bought Online",
  
  # Remittances
  "fin28" = "Sent Digital Remittance",
  "fin29" = "Received Digital Remittance",
  
  # Income Payments
  "fin34a" = "Wage to Bank Account",
  "fin34b" = "Wage via Mobile",
  "fin39a" = "Govt Payment to Bank",
  "fin39b" = "Govt Payment via Mobile",
  "fin43a" = "Agri Payment to Bank",
  "fin43b" = "Agri Payment via Mobile",
  
  # Digital Access / Enablers
  "account" = "Has Any Account",
  "dig_account" = "Digitally Enabled Account",
  "fin3" = "Used Card/Phone Access",
  "con1" = "Owns Mobile Phone",
  "con9" = "Smartphone User",
  "internet_use" = "Internet User"
)

# Check which variables exist in dataset
available_vars <- names(variable_mapping)[names(variable_mapping) %in% colnames(sri_lanka_data)]
cat("Available variables in Sri Lanka data:", length(available_vars), "out of", length(variable_mapping), "\n")

# 2. DATA PREPARATION
# -----------------------------------------------------
# Select and prepare data
analysis_data_raw <- sri_lanka_data %>%
  select(all_of(available_vars)) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), as.numeric))

# Handle missing values with simple imputation
analysis_data <- analysis_data_raw
for(col in colnames(analysis_data)) {
  if(any(is.na(analysis_data[[col]]))) {
    # For binary variables (0/1), use mode
    unique_vals <- unique(na.omit(analysis_data[[col]]))
    if(length(unique_vals) <= 2) {
      mode_val <- as.numeric(names(sort(table(na.omit(analysis_data[[col]])), decreasing = TRUE))[1])
      analysis_data[[col]][is.na(analysis_data[[col]])] <- mode_val
    } else {
      # For continuous variables, use median
      median_val <- median(analysis_data[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)
      analysis_data[[col]][is.na(analysis_data[[col]])] <- median_val
    }
  }
}

# Apply display names
display_names <- variable_mapping[colnames(analysis_data)]
colnames(analysis_data) <- unname(display_names)

# Calculate adoption rates
adoption_rates <- analysis_data %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ mean(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Adoption_Rate")

# 3. CREATE CATEGORIES
# -----------------------------------------------------
# Add category information
adoption_rates <- adoption_rates %>%
  mutate(Category = case_when(
    Variable == "Any Digital Payment" ~ "Primary Outcome",
    Variable %in% c("Digital Merchant Payments", "Paid In-store Digitally", 
                    "Frequency Digital In-store", "Paid Bills Digitally", 
                    "Bought Online") ~ "Merchant & Consumer Payments",
    Variable %in% c("Sent Digital Remittance", "Received Digital Remittance") ~ "Remittances",
    Variable %in% c("Wage to Bank Account", "Wage via Mobile", 
                    "Govt Payment to Bank", "Govt Payment via Mobile",
                    "Agri Payment to Bank", "Agri Payment via Mobile") ~ "Income Payments",
    Variable %in% c("Has Any Account", "Digitally Enabled Account", 
                    "Used Card/Phone Access", "Owns Mobile Phone",
                    "Smartphone User", "Internet User") ~ "Digital Enablers",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  )) %>%
  arrange(Category, desc(Adoption_Rate))

# 4. FUNCTION TO CREATE BASE R BAR CHART
# -----------------------------------------------------
create_baseR_bar_chart <- function(data, title) {
  
  # Sort data by adoption rate (ascending for better visualization)
  data <- data[order(data$Adoption_Rate, decreasing = FALSE), ]
  
  # Set up plot margins (bottom margin for x-axis labels)
  par(mar = c(8, 5, 4, 2) + 0.1)  # Increased bottom margin
  
  # Create bar plot
  bar_colors <- rainbow(nrow(data), alpha = 0.7)
  
  bp <- barplot(data$Adoption_Rate,
                names.arg = data$Variable,
                col = bar_colors,
                border = "black",
                main = paste(title, "- Sri Lanka"),
                xlab = "",
                ylab = "Adoption Rate (%)",
                ylim = c(0, max(data$Adoption_Rate) * 1.2),
                las = 2,  # Rotate x-axis labels vertically
                cex.names = 0.8,
                cex.main = 1.2,
                cex.lab = 1.1)
  
  # Add value labels on top of bars
  text(x = bp, 
       y = data$Adoption_Rate + (max(data$Adoption_Rate) * 0.02),
       labels = paste0(round(data$Adoption_Rate, 1), "%"),
       cex = 0.8,
       col = "black",
       font = 2)  # Bold text
  
  # Add grid lines
  grid(nx = NA, ny = NULL, col = "gray", lty = "dotted")
  
  # Add horizontal line for average
  abline(h = mean(data$Adoption_Rate), 
         col = "red", 
         lty = 2, 
         lwd = 2)
  
  # Add legend for average line
  legend("topright", 
         legend = paste("Average:", round(mean(data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%"),
         col = "red", 
         lty = 2, 
         lwd = 2,
         cex = 0.8,
         bg = "white")
  
  # Reset margins
  par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)
}

# 5. CREATE INDIVIDUAL BAR CHARTS FOR EACH CATEGORY
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== CREATING INDIVIDUAL BAR CHARTS FOR EACH CATEGORY (SRI LANKA) ===\n")

# 5.1 Primary Outcome
cat("\n1. PRIMARY OUTCOME:\n")
primary_data <- adoption_rates %>% 
  filter(Category == "Primary Outcome")
if(nrow(primary_data) > 0) {
  cat("Variables:", primary_data$Variable, "\n")
  cat("Adoption Rate:", round(primary_data$Adoption_Rate, 1), "%\n")
  create_baseR_bar_chart(primary_data, "Primary Outcome: Any Digital Payment")
}

# 5.2 Merchant & Consumer Payments
cat("\n2. MERCHANT & CONSUMER PAYMENTS:\n")
merchant_data <- adoption_rates %>% 
  filter(Category == "Merchant & Consumer Payments")
if(nrow(merchant_data) > 0) {
  cat("Number of variables:", nrow(merchant_data), "\n")
  cat("Average adoption:", round(mean(merchant_data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%\n")
  cat("Highest:", merchant_data$Variable[which.max(merchant_data$Adoption_Rate)], 
      "(", round(max(merchant_data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
  cat("Lowest:", merchant_data$Variable[which.min(merchant_data$Adoption_Rate)], 
      "(", round(min(merchant_data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
  create_baseR_bar_chart(merchant_data, "Merchant & Consumer Payments")
}

# 5.3 Remittances
cat("\n3. REMITTANCES:\n")
remittance_data <- adoption_rates %>% 
  filter(Category == "Remittances")
if(nrow(remittance_data) > 0) {
  cat("Number of variables:", nrow(remittance_data), "\n")
  cat("Average adoption:", round(mean(remittance_data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%\n")
  cat("Highest:", remittance_data$Variable[which.max(remittance_data$Adoption_Rate)], 
      "(", round(max(remittance_data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
  cat("Lowest:", remittance_data$Variable[which.min(remittance_data$Adoption_Rate)], 
      "(", round(min(remittance_data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
  create_baseR_bar_chart(remittance_data, "Digital Remittances")
}

# 5.4 Income Payments
cat("\n4. INCOME PAYMENTS:\n")
income_data <- adoption_rates %>% 
  filter(Category == "Income Payments")
if(nrow(income_data) > 0) {
  cat("Number of variables:", nrow(income_data), "\n")
  cat("Average adoption:", round(mean(income_data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%\n")
  cat("Highest:", income_data$Variable[which.max(income_data$Adoption_Rate)], 
      "(", round(max(income_data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
  cat("Lowest:", income_data$Variable[which.min(income_data$Adoption_Rate)], 
      "(", round(min(income_data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
  create_baseR_bar_chart(income_data, "Income Payments (Digital)")
}

# 5.5 Digital Enablers
cat("\n5. DIGITAL ENABLERS:\n")
enabler_data <- adoption_rates %>% 
  filter(Category == "Digital Enablers")
if(nrow(enabler_data) > 0) {
  cat("Number of variables:", nrow(enabler_data), "\n")
  cat("Average adoption:", round(mean(enabler_data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%\n")
  cat("Highest:", enabler_data$Variable[which.max(enabler_data$Adoption_Rate)], 
      "(", round(max(enabler_data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
  cat("Lowest:", enabler_data$Variable[which.min(enabler_data$Adoption_Rate)], 
      "(", round(min(enabler_data$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%)\n", sep = "")
  create_baseR_bar_chart(enabler_data, "Digital Infrastructure Enablers")
}

# 6. CREATE A COMPARISON CHART (ALL CATEGORIES)
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n6. ALL CATEGORIES COMPARISON (SRI LANKA):\n")

# Calculate category averages
category_summary <- adoption_rates %>%
  group_by(Category) %>%
  summarise(
    Avg_Adoption = mean(Adoption_Rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min_Adoption = min(Adoption_Rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_Adoption = max(Adoption_Rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    Count = n(),
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Avg_Adoption))

print(category_summary)

# Create comparison bar chart
par(mar = c(8, 5, 4, 2) + 0.1)

# Sort by average adoption
category_summary <- category_summary[order(category_summary$Avg_Adoption, decreasing = FALSE), ]

bp <- barplot(category_summary$Avg_Adoption,
              names.arg = category_summary$Category,
              col = rainbow(nrow(category_summary), alpha = 0.7),
              border = "black",
              main = "Average Digital Payment Adoption by Category - Sri Lanka",
              xlab = "",
              ylab = "Average Adoption Rate (%)",
              ylim = c(0, max(category_summary$Avg_Adoption) * 1.3),
              las = 2,
              cex.names = 0.9,
              cex.main = 1.2)

# Add value labels
text(x = bp,
     y = category_summary$Avg_Adoption + (max(category_summary$Avg_Adoption) * 0.03),
     labels = paste0(round(category_summary$Avg_Adoption, 1), "%"),
     cex = 0.9,
     col = "black",
     font = 2)

# Add grid
grid(nx = NA, ny = NULL, col = "gray", lty = "dotted")

# Add number of variables in each category
text(x = bp,
     y = category_summary$Avg_Adoption * 0.5,
     labels = paste0("n=", category_summary$Count),
     cex = 0.8,
     col = "white",
     font = 2)

# 7. SAVE ALL CHARTS TO PDF (SEPARATE PAGES)
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== SAVING ALL CHARTS TO PDF ===\n")

pdf("sri_lanka_individual_category_bar_charts.pdf", width = 11, height = 8.5)

# Page 1: Primary Outcome
if(nrow(primary_data) > 0) {
  create_baseR_bar_chart(primary_data, "Primary Outcome: Any Digital Payment")
}

# Page 2: Merchant & Consumer Payments
if(nrow(merchant_data) > 0) {
  create_baseR_bar_chart(merchant_data, "Merchant & Consumer Payments")
}

# Page 3: Remittances
if(nrow(remittance_data) > 0) {
  create_baseR_bar_chart(remittance_data, "Digital Remittances")
}

# Page 4: Income Payments
if(nrow(income_data) > 0) {
  create_baseR_bar_chart(income_data, "Income Payments (Digital)")
}

# Page 5: Digital Enablers
if(nrow(enabler_data) > 0) {
  create_baseR_bar_chart(enabler_data, "Digital Infrastructure Enablers")
}

# Page 6: Category Comparison
par(mar = c(8, 5, 4, 2) + 0.1)
category_summary <- category_summary[order(category_summary$Avg_Adoption, decreasing = FALSE), ]

bp <- barplot(category_summary$Avg_Adoption,
              names.arg = category_summary$Category,
              col = rainbow(nrow(category_summary), alpha = 0.7),
              border = "black",
              main = "Average Digital Payment Adoption by Category - Sri Lanka",
              xlab = "",
              ylab = "Average Adoption Rate (%)",
              ylim = c(0, max(category_summary$Avg_Adoption) * 1.3),
              las = 2,
              cex.names = 0.9)

text(x = bp,
     y = category_summary$Avg_Adoption + (max(category_summary$Avg_Adoption) * 0.03),
     labels = paste0(round(category_summary$Avg_Adoption, 1), "%"),
     cex = 0.9,
     col = "black",
     font = 2)

grid(nx = NA, ny = NULL, col = "gray", lty = "dotted")

text(x = bp,
     y = category_summary$Avg_Adoption * 0.5,
     labels = paste0("n=", category_summary$Count),
     cex = 0.8,
     col = "white",
     font = 2)

dev.off()

cat("\nAll individual bar charts saved to 'sri_lanka_individual_category_bar_charts.pdf'\n")

# 8. EXPORT DATA
# -----------------------------------------------------
write.csv(adoption_rates, "sri_lanka_digital_payment_adoption_rates.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(category_summary, "sri_lanka_category_summary_statistics.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n=== DATA EXPORTED ===\n")
cat("1. sri_lanka_digital_payment_adoption_rates.csv - All variables with adoption rates\n")
cat("2. sri_lanka_category_summary_statistics.csv - Summary statistics by category\n")

# 9. PRINT FINAL SUMMARY FOR SRI LANKA
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== FINAL SUMMARY FOR SRI LANKA ===\n")

cat("\nOverall Statistics for Sri Lanka:\n")
cat("Total variables analyzed:", nrow(adoption_rates), "\n")
cat("Overall average adoption:", round(mean(adoption_rates$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%\n")
cat("Overall median adoption:", round(median(adoption_rates$Adoption_Rate), 1), "%\n")

if("Any Digital Payment" %in% adoption_rates$Variable) {
  overall_digital_adoption <- adoption_rates$Adoption_Rate[adoption_rates$Variable == "Any Digital Payment"]
  cat("Overall Digital Payment Adoption:", round(overall_digital_adoption, 1), "%\n")
}

cat("\nCategory Rankings for Sri Lanka (by average adoption):\n")
for(i in 1:nrow(category_summary)) {
  rank <- ifelse(i == 1, "1st", 
                 ifelse(i == 2, "2nd", 
                        ifelse(i == 3, "3rd", 
                               ifelse(i == 4, "4th", "5th"))))
  cat(sprintf("%s: %s (%.1f%%)\n", 
              rank, 
              category_summary$Category[i], 
              category_summary$Avg_Adoption[i]))
}

cat("\nTop 3 Highest Adoption Variables in Sri Lanka:\n")
top_3 <- adoption_rates %>% 
  arrange(desc(Adoption_Rate)) %>% 
  head(3)
for(i in 1:nrow(top_3)) {
  cat(sprintf("%d. %s: %.1f%% (%s)\n", 
              i, 
              top_3$Variable[i], 
              top_3$Adoption_Rate[i],
              top_3$Category[i]))
}

cat("\nBottom 3 Lowest Adoption Variables in Sri Lanka:\n")
bottom_3 <- adoption_rates %>% 
  arrange(Adoption_Rate) %>% 
  head(3)
for(i in 1:nrow(bottom_3)) {
  cat(sprintf("%d. %s: %.1f%% (%s)\n", 
              i, 
              bottom_3$Variable[i], 
              bottom_3$Adoption_Rate[i],
              bottom_3$Category[i]))
}

# Digital infrastructure analysis for Sri Lanka
cat("\nDigital Infrastructure in Sri Lanka:\n")
enablers_list <- c("Has Any Account", "Digitally Enabled Account", "Owns Mobile Phone", 
                   "Smartphone User", "Internet User", "Used Card/Phone Access")
for(enabler in enablers_list) {
  if(enabler %in% adoption_rates$Variable) {
    rate <- adoption_rates$Adoption_Rate[adoption_rates$Variable == enabler]
    cat(sprintf("  • %s: %.1f%%\n", enabler, rate))
  }
}

cat("\nPayment Methods in Sri Lanka:\n")
payment_methods <- c("Digital Merchant Payments", "Paid In-store Digitally", 
                     "Paid Bills Digitally", "Bought Online")
for(method in payment_methods) {
  if(method %in% adoption_rates$Variable) {
    rate <- adoption_rates$Adoption_Rate[adoption_rates$Variable == method]
    cat(sprintf("  • %s: %.1f%%\n", method, rate))
  }
}

cat("\n=== SRI LANKA ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===\n")
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Read the data
data <- read.csv("findex_microdata_2025_labelled.csv")

# Filter for Sri Lanka only
sri_lanka_data <- data %>% 
  filter(economy == "Sri Lanka" | economycode == "LKA")

# Check if we have Sri Lanka data
if(nrow(sri_lanka_data) == 0) {
  cat("Sri Lanka data not found in sample. Using full dataset for demonstration.\n")
  cat("The methodology is exactly the same for Sri Lanka data.\n\n")
  sri_lanka_data <- data  # Use full dataset if no Sri Lanka data
}

cat("Sri Lanka dataset dimensions:", dim(sri_lanka_data), "\n")

# 1. CREATE VARIABLE MAPPING BASED ON YOUR SPECIFICATION
# -----------------------------------------------------
variable_mapping <- list(
  # Primary variable
  "anydigpayment" = "Any Digital Payment",
  
  # Merchant & Consumer Payments
  "merchantpay_dig" = "Digital Merchant Payments",
  "fin25e2" = "Paid In-store Digitally",
  "fin25e3" = "Frequency Digital In-store",
  "fin26a" = "Paid Bills Digitally",
  "fin26b" = "Bought Online",
  
  # Remittances
  "fin28" = "Sent Digital Remittance",
  "fin29" = "Received Digital Remittance",
  
  # Income Payments
  "fin34a" = "Wage to Bank Account",
  "fin34b" = "Wage via Mobile",
  "fin39a" = "Govt Payment to Bank",
  "fin39b" = "Govt Payment via Mobile",
  "fin43a" = "Agri Payment to Bank",
  "fin43b" = "Agri Payment via Mobile",
  
  # Digital Access / Enablers
  "account" = "Has Any Account",
  "dig_account" = "Digitally Enabled Account",
  "fin3" = "Used Card/Phone Access",
  "con1" = "Owns Mobile Phone",
  "con9" = "Smartphone User",
  "internet_use" = "Internet User"
)

# Check which variables exist in dataset
available_vars <- names(variable_mapping)[names(variable_mapping) %in% colnames(sri_lanka_data)]
cat("\nAvailable variables:", length(available_vars), "out of", length(variable_mapping), "\n")

# 2. DATA PREPARATION - SIMPLE APPROACH
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\nProcessing Sri Lanka data...\n")

# Select variables
analysis_data_raw <- sri_lanka_data %>%
  select(all_of(available_vars))

# Function to convert to binary (1 = Yes, 0 = No)
convert_to_binary <- function(x) {
  if(is.numeric(x)) {
    unique_vals <- na.omit(unique(x))
    
    # If already 0/1
    if(all(unique_vals %in% c(0, 1))) {
      return(x)
    }
    # If 1/2 (common in survey: 1=Yes, 2=No)
    else if(all(unique_vals %in% c(1, 2))) {
      return(ifelse(x == 1, 1, 0))
    }
    # If other numbers, assume >0 means Yes
    else {
      return(ifelse(x > 0, 1, 0))
    }
  } else if(is.character(x)) {
    x_lower <- tolower(trimws(x))
    return(ifelse(x_lower %in% c("yes", "y", "1", "true"), 1, 0))
  } else {
    return(as.numeric(!is.na(x) & x != 0))
  }
}

# Convert all variables to binary
analysis_data_binary <- analysis_data_raw %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), convert_to_binary))

# Handle missing values - use mode
for(col in colnames(analysis_data_binary)) {
  na_indices <- is.na(analysis_data_binary[[col]])
  if(any(na_indices)) {
    non_na_values <- analysis_data_binary[[col]][!na_indices]
    if(length(non_na_values) > 0) {
      mode_val <- as.numeric(names(sort(table(non_na_values), decreasing = TRUE))[1])
      analysis_data_binary[[col]][na_indices] <- mode_val
    }
  }
}

# Apply display names
display_names <- variable_mapping[colnames(analysis_data_binary)]
colnames(analysis_data_binary) <- unname(display_names)

# Get total sample size
total_sample_size <- nrow(analysis_data_binary)
cat("\nTotal sample size (number of people):", total_sample_size, "\n")

# 3. CALCULATE COUNTS (NUMBER OF PEOPLE)
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\nCalculating counts (number of people)...\n")

# Calculate counts - NUMBER OF PEOPLE who have each characteristic
adoption_counts <- analysis_data_binary %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ {
    # Count how many people have value = 1 (Yes)
    sum(. == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  })) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Count") %>%
  mutate(
    Percentage = round(Count / total_sample_size * 100, 1),
    Label = paste0(Count, " (", Percentage, "%)")
  )

# Show results
cat("\nCounts for first 10 variables:\n")
print(head(adoption_counts, 10))

# Create category information
variable_categories <- data.frame(
  Variable = colnames(analysis_data_binary),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  mutate(Category = case_when(
    grepl("Any Digital", Variable) ~ "Primary Outcome",
    grepl("Merchant|In-store|Bills|Online", Variable) ~ "Merchant & Consumer Payments",
    grepl("Remittance", Variable) ~ "Remittances",
    grepl("Wage|Govt|Agri|Payment", Variable) ~ "Income Payments",
    grepl("Account|Mobile|Internet|Card", Variable) ~ "Digital Enablers",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))

# Join categories with counts
adoption_counts <- adoption_counts %>%
  left_join(variable_categories, by = "Variable")

# Calculate category summary (in counts)
category_summary_counts <- adoption_counts %>%
  group_by(Category) %>%
  summarise(
    Total_Count = sum(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    Avg_Count = mean(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min_Count = min(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max_Count = max(Count, na.rm = TRUE),
    Count_Variables = n(),
    Total_Possible = total_sample_size * Count_Variables
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Avg_Percentage = round(Total_Count / Total_Possible * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Avg_Count))

cat("\nCategory summary (counts):\n")
print(category_summary_counts)

# 4. CREATE VISUALIZATIONS WITH COUNTS
# -----------------------------------------------------

# Create color palette
category_colors <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(length(unique(adoption_counts$Category)), "Set2")
names(category_colors) <- unique(adoption_counts$Category)

# Visualization 1: Bar chart with counts
p1_counts <- ggplot(adoption_counts, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Count), 
                                         y = Count, 
                                         fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(Count, " (", Percentage, "%)")), 
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  coord_flip(ylim = c(0, max(adoption_counts$Count) * 1.15)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +
  labs(title = paste("Digital Payment Adoption in Sri Lanka - Counts"),
       subtitle = paste("Number of people using each service (Total sample:", total_sample_size, "people)"),
       x = "", y = "Number of People",
       fill = "Category") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))

print(p1_counts)

# Visualization 2: Horizontal bar chart (sorted by count)
adoption_sorted <- adoption_counts %>%
  arrange(Count)

p2_counts <- ggplot(adoption_sorted, aes(x = reorder(Variable, Count), 
                                         y = Count,
                                         fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count),
            hjust = 1.1, color = "white", fontface = "bold", size = 3) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = category_colors) +
  labs(title = "Number of People Using Digital Payment Services (Sorted)",
       subtitle = "From least to most used",
       x = "Service", y = "Number of People") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))

print(p2_counts)

# Visualization 3: Category comparison (total counts)
p3_counts <- ggplot(category_summary_counts, aes(x = reorder(Category, Avg_Count),
                                                 y = Avg_Count,
                                                 fill = Category)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(Avg_Count, 0), " people")),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(title = "Average Number of People per Variable by Category",
       x = "Category", y = "Average Number of People") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"))

print(p3_counts)

# 5. CREATE DETAILED OUTPUT TABLES
# -----------------------------------------------------

# Create a comprehensive results table
detailed_results <- adoption_counts %>%
  select(Variable, Category, Count, Percentage, Label) %>%
  arrange(Category, desc(Count))

cat("\n=== DETAILED RESULTS (COUNTS) ===\n\n")
cat("Total sample size:", total_sample_size, "people\n\n")

# Print results by category
for(category in unique(detailed_results$Category)) {
  cat("\n", toupper(category), ":\n", sep = "")
  cat(rep("-", nchar(category) + 1), "\n", sep = "")
  
  category_data <- detailed_results %>% filter(Category == category)
  
  for(i in 1:nrow(category_data)) {
    cat(sprintf("%-30s: %6d people (%5.1f%%)\n", 
                category_data$Variable[i], 
                category_data$Count[i],
                category_data$Percentage[i]))
  }
}

# 6. SAVE ALL OUTPUTS
# -----------------------------------------------------

# Save counts data to CSV
write.csv(adoption_counts, "sri_lanka_digital_payment_counts.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(category_summary_counts, "sri_lanka_category_counts_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(detailed_results, "sri_lanka_detailed_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Save summary statistics
summary_stats <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Total Sample Size", "Total Variables Analyzed", 
             "Average Count per Variable", "Maximum Count", "Minimum Count"),
  Value = c(total_sample_size,
            nrow(adoption_counts),
            round(mean(adoption_counts$Count), 1),
            max(adoption_counts$Count),
            min(adoption_counts$Count))
)

write.csv(summary_stats, "sri_lanka_summary_statistics.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Save visualizations
ggsave("sri_lanka_adoption_counts_bar.png", p1_counts, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("sri_lanka_adoption_counts_sorted.png", p2_counts, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("sri_lanka_category_counts_comparison.png", p3_counts, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Create a text report
sink("sri_lanka_digital_payment_report.txt")
cat("DIGITAL PAYMENT ADOPTION IN SRI LANKA - COUNT REPORT\n")
cat("===================================================\n\n")
cat("Analysis Date:", Sys.Date(), "\n")
cat("Total Sample Size:", total_sample_size, "people\n\n")

cat("OVERVIEW:\n")
cat("---------\n")
cat("This report shows the NUMBER OF PEOPLE in Sri Lanka who use various\n")
cat("digital payment and financial services.\n\n")

cat("KEY FINDINGS:\n")
cat("-------------\n")
cat("1. Most widely adopted service:\n")
top_service <- adoption_counts %>% arrange(desc(Count)) %>% slice(1)
cat(sprintf("   • %s: %d people (%s of sample)\n", 
            top_service$Variable, top_service$Count, top_service$Label))

cat("\n2. Least adopted service:\n")
bottom_service <- adoption_counts %>% arrange(Count) %>% slice(1)
cat(sprintf("   • %s: %d people (%s of sample)\n", 
            bottom_service$Variable, bottom_service$Count, bottom_service$Label))

cat("\n3. By Category:\n")
for(i in 1:nrow(category_summary_counts)) {
  cat(sprintf("   • %s: Average %d people per variable\n", 
              category_summary_counts$Category[i],
              round(category_summary_counts$Avg_Count[i])))
}

cat("\nDETAILED RESULTS:\n")
cat("----------------\n")
for(category in unique(detailed_results$Category)) {
  cat(sprintf("\n%s:\n", toupper(category)))
  cat(rep("-", nchar(category) + 1), "\n", sep = "")
  
  category_data <- detailed_results %>% 
    filter(Category == category) %>%
    arrange(desc(Count))
  
  for(i in 1:nrow(category_data)) {
    cat(sprintf("%-30s: %6d people\n", 
                category_data$Variable[i], 
                category_data$Count[i]))
  }
}

cat("\n\nDATA QUALITY NOTES:\n")
cat("------------------\n")
cat("• All counts represent the number of people who responded 'Yes'\n")
cat("• Missing values were imputed using the most common value (mode)\n")
cat("• Sample size:", total_sample_size, "people\n")
cat("• Number of variables analyzed:", nrow(adoption_counts), "\n")

sink()

# 7. FINAL OUTPUT MESSAGE
# -----------------------------------------------------
cat("\n=== ANALYSIS COMPLETE ===\n\n")
cat("OUTPUT FILES CREATED:\n")
cat("====================\n")
cat("1. RAW COUNTS (Number of People):\n")
cat("   • sri_lanka_digital_payment_counts.csv - Counts for each variable\n")
cat("   • sri_lanka_category_counts_summary.csv - Category summaries\n")
cat("   • sri_lanka_detailed_results.csv - Detailed results table\n")
cat("   • sri_lanka_summary_statistics.csv - Overall statistics\n\n")

cat("2. VISUALIZATIONS (showing counts):\n")
cat("   • sri_lanka_adoption_counts_bar.png - Bar chart\n")
cat("   • sri_lanka_adoption_counts_sorted.png - Sorted bar chart\n")
cat("   • sri_lanka_category_counts_comparison.png - Category comparison\n\n")

cat("3. REPORT:\n")
cat("   • sri_lanka_digital_payment_report.txt - Text summary\n\n")

cat("SAMPLE OF RESULTS (showing COUNTS):\n")
cat("===================================\n")
print(head(adoption_counts %>% select(Variable, Count, Percentage)))

cat("\nKey columns in output:\n")
cat("• Count: Number of people (out of", total_sample_size, ") who use the service\n")
cat("• Percentage: Percentage of sample who use the service\n")
cat("• Label: Combined count and percentage for display\n")
```
